skill.name: backlog-manager
skill.description: Manage a product backlog by clarifying ideas, merging related requests, and updating a structured backlog document.
skill.summary: Turn loose product ideas into a maintained backlog file with user confirmation.
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: Static external command findings are false positives caused by Markdown inline code and fenced template text in SKILL.md. The weak cryptography and entropy findings are also false positives; no cryptographic operation, obfuscated payload, prompt injection, network access, or command execution was found. The skill has low risk because it intentionally reads and updates a local backlog file after user confirmation.

low_findings.title: Intended Local File Modification
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to read and update docs/需求池.md as part of normal backlog management. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but users should understand it can modify a project documentation file after confirmation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file update behavior is explicit and central to the skill workflow. The same workflow requires user confirmation before writing, reducing severity.

low_findings.title: Static Findings Dismissed as Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution is Markdown inline code and a fenced document template, not executable code. The weak cryptography and high entropy findings are unsupported by the visible content in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations contain Markdown formatting, Chinese prose, and backlog template text. I found no executable shell, Ruby, cryptography calls, or encoded payload.

content.value_statement: Product ideas often arrive as scattered notes without enough context for action. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code clarify, merge, and maintain them in a structured backlog file.
actual_capabilities: Creates or updates docs/需求池.md with a structured backlog template.
Asks clarifying questions about pain points, frequency, and current workarounds.
Classifies ideas into ready, needs thought, parked, or completed sections.
Checks whether a new idea should merge with an existing backlog item.
Reviews ready items using frequency, workaround cost, and return on effort.
Archives completed items with version and completion details.
limitations: It does not create designs, PRDs, or implementation plans.
It requires user confirmation before classification, merging, or status changes.
It manages one documented backlog file and does not integrate with issue trackers.
It depends on the user to provide real pain points and decisions.

use_cases.title: Capture scattered product ideas
use_cases.description: Convert informal ideas into confirmed backlog entries with clear context and status.

use_cases.title: Prepare a focused version discussion
use_cases.description: Review ready items and compare candidates before choosing the next version scope.

use_cases.title: Clean an aging backlog
use_cases.description: Find stale, vague, or ready items and update the backlog after user approval.

prompt_templates.title: Add a new idea
prompt_templates.prompt: Use backlog-manager. I have an idea: [describe the problem or request]. Ask the needed questions before adding it.

prompt_templates.title: Review the backlog
prompt_templates.prompt: Use backlog-manager to review docs/需求池.md. Suggest stale items, items that can move forward, and questions for vague items.

prompt_templates.title: Choose next version candidates
prompt_templates.prompt: Use backlog-manager to list items that are ready. Compare them by frequency, workaround difficulty, and return on effort.

prompt_templates.title: Merge related requirements
prompt_templates.prompt: Use backlog-manager to evaluate whether this new request belongs with existing backlog items: [request]. Show merge options before editing.

output_examples.input: Users want faster exports, but I only have a vague complaint.
output_examples.output: The skill asks about the pain point, how often it happens, and the current workaround before proposing a backlog entry.

output_examples.input: Review the backlog and tell me what is ready.
output_examples.output: The skill separates ready items from vague items, explains why each item belongs there, and waits for approval before changes.

output_examples.input: Mark the bulk import item completed in version 0.8.
output_examples.output: The skill moves the item to completed, records the version, updates the overview table, and checks dependent items.

best_practices: Provide the real user pain, current workaround, and frequency when adding ideas.
Confirm every merge, category change, and batch update before allowing edits.
Review parked and vague items regularly to keep the backlog useful.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a task list for daily execution.
Do not ask it to decide the roadmap without human selection.
Do not skip clarification when a request is vague.

faq.question: What file does this skill manage?
faq.answer: It manages docs/需求池.md and uses a structured Markdown backlog format.

faq.question: Can it choose the next feature for me?
faq.answer: No. It compares candidates and explains tradeoffs, but the user makes the final choice.

faq.question: Does it create product requirements documents?
faq.answer: No. It stops when a requirement is ready for another design or PRD workflow.

faq.question: Can it merge duplicate ideas?
faq.answer: Yes. It suggests merges when items are related and waits for user confirmation.

faq.question: Is it safe for existing backlog files?
faq.answer: It is designed to update existing content in place after confirmation, but review changes before accepting them.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
